summary: Iteration 154 — SMS Unknown Numbers Inbox & Assign-Phone feature. Found and fixed 1 critical bug (unknown phones leaking into customers section). All 24 backend tests pass. All 9 UI features verified working end-to-end.

critical.issue: Unknown phones (customer_id='') were appearing in customers section when no branch_id filter was applied. The in_pipeline for sms_inbox matched ALL inbox documents (phone_filter={}) including unregistered ones.
critical.fix: Added {"customer_id": {"$ne": ""}} to in_pipeline $match in sms.py line ~738. Now only registered-customer inbox messages contribute to the customers section.

minor.issue: Still uses 'business_info' key (line 474) while settings DB has 'company_info'. From iteration_153 — not yet fixed.

issues: Pre-existing 'SYNGENTA T' appears as the phone identifier instead of a numeric phone — this is likely seeded/old test data artifact, not a code bug

action_items: Fix blast endpoint (line 474 in sms.py): change 'business_info' to 'company_info' (carried from iteration_153)
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: sms.py in_pipeline for customers section now filters out unknown phones via customer_id != ''. Previously, all sms_inbox records (including unregistered) showed up in customers section when no branch filter was applied.

handleAssignPhone in MessagesPage.js calls loadConversations('customers') AND loadConversations('unknown') in sequence. Both call setConversations() creating a potential race condition — the second call always wins since convoSection stays as 'unknown'. This is functionally correct (shows updated unknown list) but slightly confusing code. The loadConversations('customers') call is effectively a no-op from UI perspective when convoSection='unknown'. Minor code quality issue only.

Unknown section: SYNGENTA T appears as phone identifier in Unknown section — suggests some old sms_inbox record has 'phone' field set to a name string. This is data integrity issue from old test seeds.
After assign, the phone correctly disappears from unknown section and its conversation moves to customers section with the assigned customer's name and branch.
seed_data_creation: Created test inbox messages via POST /sms/inbox for phones: 09700000001, 09700000002, 09700000003, 09700000098, 09700000099, +639700000003. Assigned 09700000003 and 09700000098/09700000099 to known customers during tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: SMS Unknown Numbers feature fully tested and working. Critical bug fixed: sms.py in_pipeline now filters unknown phones from customers section. Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_sms_unknown_inbox_154.py (24 tests). UI features: section toggle, amber avatars, Unregistered badge, Assign modal, branch name in search results — all working. Branch 1 ID: c435277f-9fc7-4d83-83e7-38be5b4423ac. Branch 2 ID: 18c02daa-bce0-45de-860a-70ccc6ed6c6d.

mocked_apis.note: Physical SMS dispatch is MOCKED — messages are queued but not actually sent via SIM/Android gateway.

rca_of_bug.bug: Unknown phones appearing in customers section
rca_of_bug.root_cause: In list_conversations() (sms.py ~line 737), the in_pipeline for the customers section used phone_filter = {} (empty dict when no branch_id provided), which matched ALL documents in sms_inbox including unregistered ones. These unknown phones were then added as new entries in the merged dict (since they had no matching outgoing queue messages).
reproduction_steps: 1. POST /sms/inbox with a completely unknown phone (no matching customer)
3. Unknown phone appears in response with customer_id='', customer_name=phone number
rca_of_bug.fix: Added {"customer_id": {"$ne": ""}} to the in_pipeline $match. This ensures only inbox records that were matched to a customer (customer_id != '') contribute to the customers section conversation list.
